recommendations.action: Accept WAIVED posture — six P2 ACs cover a configuration-surface substrate story with no live test runner. AC 1 + AC 6 have STRONG substrate evidence: Task 7 six-smoke bundle covers static JSON validity (smoke 1), single-bundled-mode shape invariance (smoke 2), config-manifest key parity (smoke 3), manifest-version parity (smoke 4), 20-invariant manifest-load (smoke 5), and sync-gate clean (smoke 6). All six smokes re-verified LIVE at iter-78 trace-time. AC 2 + AC 3 + AC 4 + AC 5 are downstream Story-13.5 consumer ACs explicitly carved out of Story 1.14 scope; Story 13.5's .github/workflows/release-please.yml (Epic 13) is the runtime consumer. Per-AC runner-hosted coverage deferred to Story 1.16.

Story 1.14 authors the release-please config substrate as a contract (drift-detected by Story 1.9 sync-gate via 3 new manifest entries `INV-release-please-config` + `INV-release-please-manifest` + `INV-release-please-rationale` at content-hash level). Any silent edit to the two JSON files or the rationale markdown triggers sync-gate FAIL at pre-merge. Story 13.5 consumes this contract at runtime; until Story 13.5 lands no Release PR is emitted (zero runtime behaviour at Story 1.14 substrate stage — consistent with AC 5 scope carve-out).

Story 1.16 test-runner will unlock optional per-AC probes: AC 1 JSON-schema validation against release-please canonical config.json schema (`$schema` reference at config top); AC 2/3/4 release-please bump-classification unit test with commitlint-conventional stub inputs; AC 5 release-please dry-run integration (`npx release-please release-pr --dry-run`); AC 6 markdown-link checker + architecture.md:1342 verbatim-pointer assertion. None block Story 1.14 `review → done` transition.
Run /bmad-testarch-test-review to assess test quality (no tests exist — no-op; recorded for parity with downstream pipelines).
